operating_state: Total revenue increased 16% to $52.6 billion driven by 15% comparable sales growth and 20 net new warehouses opened since prior year. Gasoline price inflation contributed 511 basis points to sales growth through higher average prices and increased volumes. Membership fee revenue increased 9% driven by new sign-ups, Executive membership upgrades, and renewal rates of 92% in U.S./Canada and 90% worldwide.
cost_structure: Gross margin percentage decreased 99 basis points to 10.19% primarily from core merchandise pressures in fresh foods and non-foods categories, plus LIFO charges reflecting higher merchandise costs. SG&A expenses as percentage of sales decreased 84 basis points to 8.62% through leveraging increased sales volumes, despite wage increases instituted in October 2021 and March 2022.
material_events: Board declared quarterly dividend increase to $0.90 per share compared to $0.79 in prior year. Company repurchased $139 million of common stock with $2.99 billion remaining under authorization expiring April 2023. Russ Miller was promoted to Senior Executive Vice President, U.S. Operations effective May 4, 2022.
macro_exposures: Foreign currency headwinds negatively impacted net sales by $476 million primarily from Other International operations. Merchandise costs were impacted by inflation higher than recent years, with the company employing strategies including supplier cost-sharing, early purchasing in greater volumes, and chartering container vessels. COVID-related supply and logistics constraints continued affecting merchandise categories while tariff actions created uncertainty regarding merchandise costs.
forward_outlook: Management expects COVID-related supply and logistics constraints to continue affecting merchandise categories while substantially higher LIFO charges are expected in Q4 2022 due to continued merchandise cost inflation. The company plans to open 10 additional new warehouses in remainder of fiscal 2022 with capital expenditures financed through operating cash flows and existing liquidity.
uncertainty_profile: The company faces multiple legal proceedings including California Labor Code violations, wage and hour claims, and opioid litigation, though management believes pending litigation will not have material adverse effects on financial position. Company implemented new financial planning and reporting application during Q3 requiring changes to certain business processes and internal controls.
